summary: The session focused on how to use SharePoint to reference content on a network share without ingesting it, including creating links and content sources, and managing user access to indexed content. The user sought detailed instructions on these processes and how to ensure proper permissions are set for sensitive content.
facts: The user inquired about using SharePoint to refer to content available on a network share without ingesting it. The user asked how to create a link to a network share location in a SharePoint library or page. The user requested instructions on creating a content source in SharePoint. The user sought methods to prevent unauthorized access to content that has been crawled and indexed by SharePoint. SharePoint allows setting permissions to restrict access to content based on user roles. Item-level permissions in SharePoint can control access to specific content within a library or list. Result sources in SharePoint can filter search results based on defined criteria. SharePoint Information Rights Management (IRM) can protect sensitive information by applying usage rights and permissions. SharePoint Secure Store Service (SSS) stores and maps credentials for connecting to external systems.
